cards.front: What is an ordinary variable in shell programming?
cards.back: An ordinary variable is a variable that holds a value assigned to it by the user using the assignment operator (=).

cards.front: What is an environment variable in shell programming?
cards.back: An environment variable is a variable that holds a value set by the operating system or a shell script.

cards.front: How do you print the value of an environment variable in shell programming?
Can ordinary variables be set in shell programming?
cards.back: Yes, ordinary variables can be set using the assignment operator (=) or by setting a default value using the declare -A command.

cards.front: How do you set a default value for an ordinary variable in shell programming?
cards.back: You can set a default value for an ordinary variable using the declare -A command or by assigning a default value in the shell script.
